title: Conversion Funnel Analysis
business_definition: A conversion funnel tracks the percentage of users who complete each step toward a goal (e.g. visit → signup → activation → payment). Funnel analysis finds where and why users drop off.
diagnostic_questions: Which funnel step has the worst conversion rate and when did it start degrading?
Did a specific traffic source, device type, or geography drive the conversion drop?
Did any product change or A/B test launch coincide with the change?
Is the absolute volume of top-of-funnel traffic also changing, or just conversion rate?

causal_relationships.detection_sql: SELECT step, COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) AS users, COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) * 100.0 / FIRST_VALUE(COUNT(DISTINCT user_id)) OVER (ORDER BY step_order) AS conversion_pct FROM funnel_events WHERE event_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days' GROUP BY step, step_order ORDER BY step_order

SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', trial_start) AS cohort, COUNT(*) AS trials, COUNT(CASE WHEN converted_at IS NOT NULL THEN 1 END) AS converted, COUNT(CASE WHEN converted_at IS NOT NULL THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0) AS conversion_rate FROM trials GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

inflation_causes.cause: Bot or automated traffic inflating top-of-funnel without converting
inflation_causes.detection_sql: SELECT traffic_source, COUNT(*) AS sessions, SUM(CASE WHEN converted THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS conversions FROM sessions GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 2 DESC

deflation_causes.cause: Session tracking gap — users who converted not attributed due to cookie loss
deflation_causes.detection_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS conversions_without_session FROM orders o LEFT JOIN sessions s ON o.session_id = s.session_id WHERE s.session_id IS NULL

anti_patterns: Do not compare funnel conversion across different traffic sources without segmenting — intent quality varies wildly
Do not measure funnel conversion within a single day — use cohort windows (e.g. 7-day or 30-day conversion rate)

sql_assets.funnel_by_step: SELECT step_name, step_order, COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) AS users, LAG(COUNT(DISTINCT user_id)) OVER (ORDER BY step_order) AS prev_step_users, COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) * 100.0 / NULLIF(LAG(COUNT(DISTINCT user_id)) OVER (ORDER BY step_order), 0) AS step_conversion FROM funnel_events GROUP BY step_name, step_order ORDER BY step_order
